title: VDD static_analysis: run_chainlink_create uses shell interpolation — command injection via finding title
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access (OWASP A03 Injection). Location: src/vdd/static_analysis.rs:80-129 (run_chainlink_create). Violation: The function builds a shell command via format! and executes through sh -c. The title originates from adversary-controlled text (finding.description, truncated). An adversary LLM returning a finding with backticks, dollar-paren, semicolons, or newlines breaks out of quoting. The .replace quote pseudo-sanitization does NOT neutralize backticks, dollar, semicolons, or newlines under sh -c. Same bug applies to label and comment commands on L112 and L119-L125. Adversary JSON is attacker-controlled surface by design. Mandated Refactor: Replace shell invocation with tokio::process::Command::new(chainlink).arg(create).arg(title).arg(-p).arg(high) — argv-level arg passing eliminates shell interpretation. Do the same for label and comment subcommands. Delete the .replace pseudo-sanitization.
